name: cpu_llm_infer
title: Local language model on CPU
short_description: Token generation from a small quantised language model on the CPU.

structured_text.description: Generates text from a small quantised language model running entirely on the processor, the way local assistants run on machines without a capable GPU. The model, prompt and number of tokens are fixed, and the work is largely bound by how fast the CPU can move and multiply the model weights.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the number of tokens generated per second during steady output, reported in tokens per second.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. More tokens per second means a more responsive local assistant, since text appears closer to reading speed.
structured_text.reasoning: Running language models locally is now a mainstream use, and CPU inference is bound by memory bandwidth and vector math in a way that reflects real assistant performance.

domain_gaming.explanation: Some games experiment with local language models for dynamic dialogue.

domain_office.explanation: Local writing and summarising assistants are increasingly part of office work.

domain_software_development.explanation: Local coding assistants run language models on the developer's own machine.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Design tools add local language helpers for captions and copy.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Editors use local models for transcripts and summaries.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Rendering pipelines make little use of local language models.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work touches language models mainly for transcription.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Engineering assistants are emerging but not yet central.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Researchers use local models as writing and analysis aids.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Quick CPU checks of a model are common during training work.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Local language model serving on the CPU is exactly this domain.

domain_data_science.explanation: Analysts run local models to label, extract and summarise text data.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Cost sensitive deployments serve smaller models from CPU only servers.
